HOME_HERO_SUBTITLE: A modern Twitch song request overlay and Spotify integration app for streamers.
HOME_HERO_DESC: Built with React and Electron, Request+ provides a smooth desktop experience for seamless, real-time song requests integrated directly with Spotify, YouTube Music, and Apple Music.
HOME_NOTICE_BODY: We are reworking how account logins and server data are used to minimize code size for most systems. Our Terms and Privacy Policy will be updated accordingly with these changes. We are committed to transparency with the open-source community. Request+ will always be free, but some future features may be paid add-ons to cover development costs. Supporters can make features free for all.
HOME_FEATURES_SUBTITLE: Everything you need to manage song requests and keep your stream interactive.
HOME_TECH_SUBTITLE: Request+ leverages the best tools for a fast, reliable streaming experience.
HOME_CTA_TITLE: Ready to Level Up Your Stream?
HOME_CTA_SUBTITLE: Join thousands of streamers using Request+ for seamless song requests. It's free, open-source, and always will be.

HOME_FEAT_CHAT_DESC: Viewers can request songs from both Twitch and Kick using simple chat commands.
HOME_FEAT_OVERLAY_DESC: Add a visual overlay to OBS or Streamlabs to display "Now Playing."
HOME_FEAT_AUTH_DESC: Secure account authentication with OAuth for Twitch and Kick integrations.
HOME_FEAT_UI_DESC: React + Electron for fast, minimal system resource usage.
HOME_FEAT_QUEUE_DESC: Skip, reorder, and manage incoming song requests with ease.

FAQ_SUBTITLE: Find answers to common questions about Request+.
FAQ_CONTACT_DESC: Join our Discord community to get help from other users and the development team.
FAQ_Q1: Is Request+ affiliated with Twitch or Spotify?
FAQ_A1: No, Request+ is an independent project and is not endorsed or affiliated with Twitch, Spotify, or Spicetify.
FAQ_A2: Yes! You can edit its CSS or use a template theme to customize the overlay however you like. Request+ provides several pre-built themes and supports full CSS customization for advanced users.
FAQ_Q3: What happens if Spotify or Twitch APIs go down?
FAQ_A3: Request+ won't be able to fetch or play new songs until the APIs recover. The application will continue to run, but functionality dependent on those services will be temporarily unavailable.
FAQ_A4: Yes — only basic authentication tokens are stored, and they're securely managed. Request+ does not store Spotify playback history or personal data. Only Twitch and Kick usernames and tokens are temporarily stored. See our Privacy Policy for more details.
FAQ_A5: Yes! Request+ is completely free and open-source with no premium features or paywalls. Some future features may be paid add-ons to cover development costs, but the core functionality will always remain free. Supporters can also make paid features free for all users.
FAQ_Q6: Do I need a Twitch account to use Request+?
FAQ_A6: No, Twitch is only a soft dependency used for song requests. You can use Request+ without a Twitch account for local playback control and overlay features. Song requests are disabled by default and can be enabled in settings.
FAQ_Q7: What music platforms does Request+ support?
FAQ_A7: Request+ supports Spotify (via Spicetify), YouTube Music (via Pear Desktop), and Apple Music (via Cider). Spicetify, Pear, or Cider must be installed separately depending on your music platform.
FAQ_Q8: Does Request+ work with Kick streams?
FAQ_A8: Yes! Request+ now supports Kick chat integration alongside Twitch. You can connect both platforms and accept song requests from viewers on either service.
FAQ_Q9: Can I use Request+ for commercial streams?
FAQ_A9: Yes, you can use Request+ for commercial purposes as long as you comply with the GNU GPLv3 license and our Terms of Service. However, you're responsible for ensuring you have the proper music licensing for your streams.
FAQ_Q10: How do I report bugs or request features?
FAQ_A10: You can report bugs and request features on our GitHub repository or join our Discord community to discuss with other users and developers.
FAQ_A11: Currently, Request+ is only available as a desktop application for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Mobile support is not planned at this time.
DOWNLOADS_SUBTITLE: Get Request+ for your platform and start accepting song requests in minutes.
DOWNLOADS_SOFT_DEPS: Twitch is only a soft dependency, required for song requests. Song requests are disabled by default and can be enabled in settings.
DOWNLOADS_HELP_DESC: Check out our comprehensive documentation for installation guides, configuration tips, and troubleshooting help.
